title: Paspalum ovatum
taxonomy: Paspalum ovatum is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. It belongs to the genus Paspalum, a group of grasses that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Paspalum ovatum is further categorized as a species within the genus Paspalum, with its exact classification as Paspalum ovatum. This species is often placed in the subfamily Panicoideae, which includes many grasses that are commonly found in warm and temperate climates. The classification of Paspalum ovatum is a way to understand its evolutionary relationships and characteristics among other plant species.

intro: Paspalum ovatum, also known as Paspalum, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Paspalum ovatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Paspalum is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
